1140 if method == "common": 1141 return round(value, precision) 1142 1143 func = getattr(math, method) 1144 return t.cast(float, func(value * (10**precision)) / (10**precision)) 1145 1146 1147 class _GroupTuple(t.NamedTuple): 1148 grouper: t.Any 1149 list: t.List 1150 1151 # Use the regular tuple repr to hide this subclass if users print 1152 # out the value during debugging. 1153 def __repr__(self) -> str: 1154 return tuple.__repr__(self) 1155 1156 def __str__(self) -> str: 1157 return tuple.__str__(self) 1158 1159 1160 @pass_environment 1161 def sync_do_groupby( 1162 environment: "Environment", 1163 value: "t.Iterable[V]", 1164 attribute: t.Union[str, int], 1165 default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, 1166 ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": 1167 """Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's 1168 :func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for 1169 nested access, like ``"address.city"``. Unlike Python's ``groupby``, 1170 the values are sorted first so only one group is returned for each 1171 unique value. 1172 1173 For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute 1174 can be rendered in groups. In this example, ``grouper`` refers to 1175 the ``city`` value of the group. 1176 1177 .. sourcecode:: html+jinja 1178 1179
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city") %} 1180
  • {{ city }} 1181
      {% for user in items %} 1182
    • {{ user.name }} 1183 {% endfor %}
    1184
    • 1185 {% endfor %}
1186 1187 ``groupby`` yields namedtuples of ``(grouper, list)``, which 1188 can be used instead of the tuple unpacking above. ``grouper`` is the 1189 value of the attribute, and ``list`` is the items with that value. 1190 1191 .. sourcecode:: html+jinja 1192 1193
    {% for group in users|groupby("city") %} 1194
  • {{ group.grouper }}: {{ group.list|join(", ") }} 1195 {% endfor %}
1196 1197 You can specify a ``default`` value to use if an object in the list 1198 does not have the given attribute. 1199 1200 .. sourcecode:: jinja 1201 1202
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city", default="NY") %} 1203
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • 1204 {% endfor %}
1205 1206 .. versionchanged:: 3.0 1207 Added the ``default`` parameter. 1208 1209 .. versionchanged:: 2.6 1210 The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. 1211 """ 1212 expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) 1213 return [ 1214 _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) 1215 for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) 1216 ] 1217 1218 1219 @async_variant(sync_do_groupby) # type: ignore 1220 async def do_groupby( 1221 environment: "Environment", 1222 value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", 1223 attribute: t.Union[str, int], 1224 default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, 1225 ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": 1226 expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) 1227 return [ 1228 _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) 1229 for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) 1230 ] 1231 1232 1233 @pass_environment 1234 def sync_do_sum( 1235 environment: "Environment", 1236 iterable: "t.Iterable[V]", 1237 attribute: t.Optional[t.Union[str, int]] = None, 1238 start: V = 0, # type: ignore 1239 ) -> V:

1219	@async_variant(sync_do_groupby)  # type: ignore
1220	async def do_groupby(
1221	    environment: "Environment",
1222	    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
1223	    attribute: t.Union[str, int],
1224	    default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None,
1225	) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]":
1226	    expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)
1227	    return [
1228	        _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values))
1229	        for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr)

diff --git a/src/jinja2/filters.py b/src/jinja2/filters.py index 80ea6504..315fa7c9 100644 --- a/src/jinja2/filters.py +++ b/src/jinja2/filters.py @@ -1157,12 +1157,27 @@ class _GroupTuple(t.NamedTuple): return tuple.__str__(self) +class _GroupTupleWithCount(t.NamedTuple): + grouper: t.Any + list: t.List + count: int + + # Use the regular tuple repr to hide this subclass if users print + # out the value during debugging. + def __repr__(self) -> str: + return tuple.__repr__(self) + + def __str__(self) -> str: + return tuple.__str__(self) + + @pass_environment def sync_do_groupby( environment: "Environment", value: "t.Iterable[V]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": """Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's :func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for @@ -1203,6 +1218,19 @@ def sync_do_groupby(
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %} + You can also pass ``with_counts=True`` to include the number of + items in each group, avoiding the need for ``|list|length`` in the + template. + + .. sourcecode:: jinja + +
      {% for city, items, count in users|groupby("city", with_counts=True) %} +
    • {{ city }} ({{ count }})
      • + {% endfor %}
    + + .. versionchanged:: 3.2 + Added the ``with_counts`` parameter. + .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. @@ -1210,6 +1238,16 @@ def sync_do_groupby( The attribute supports dot notation for nested access. """ expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + + if with_counts: + out = [] + + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr): + items = list(values) + out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, items, len(items))) + + return out + return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) @@ -1222,11 +1260,23 @@ async def do_groupby( value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + with_counts: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) + values = await auto_to_list(value) + + if with_counts: + out = [] + + for key, group in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr): + items = await auto_to_list(group) + out.append(_GroupTupleWithCount(key, items, len(items))) + + return out + return [ - _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) + _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(group)) + for key, group in groupby(sorted(values, key=expr), expr) ]

content.text:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Summary:

- Added `with_counts` parameter (default `False`) to both `sync_do_groupby` and `do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`.
- When `with_counts=True`, the filter yields `_GroupTupleWithCount` (a new `NamedTuple` with `grouper`, `list`, `count` fields) instead of the standard `_GroupTuple`, preserving backward compatibility for the default case.
- Updated docstring with usage example and a `versionchanged:: 3.2` note.
- Verified with manual sync/async renders and the full test suite (837 passed).

result:
The patch is written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. Summary:

- Added `with_counts` parameter (default `False`) to both `sync_do_groupby` and `do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`.
- When `with_counts=True`, the filter yields `_GroupTupleWithCount` (a new `NamedTuple` with `grouper`, `list`, `count` fields) instead of the standard `_GroupTuple`, preserving backward compatibility for the default case.
- Updated docstring with usage example and a `versionchanged:: 3.2` note.
- Verified with manual sync/async renders and the full test suite (837 passed).
